description: Functional workflow evals for the Contextro skill. Covers the highest-value problem-first flows: orientation, safe refactor, bug investigation, compaction recovery, AST rewrite safety, and output-handling discipline.

categories.workflow: Multi-step tool sequences happen in the right order
categories.functional: The chosen tools solve the user task correctly
categories.output_format: The agent handles confidence, sandbox_ref, and text-first outputs correctly
categories.performance_comparison: The skill reduces reads and tool chatter relative to baseline

evals.name: workflow-safe-refactor-order
evals.prompt: I want to rename BaseEmbeddingService to EmbeddingBackend across the codebase.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls impact first, then explain and/or find_callers before giving rename guidance.

assertions.passing_condition: impact('BaseEmbeddingService') called before any rename recommendation

assertions.text: response includes affected callers or files
assertions.passing_condition: Response includes specific callers or affected files from impact output

evals.name: workflow-index-then-status-then-search
evals.prompt: Index /Users/alice/myapp and then find all the database connection logic.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent performs index, waits on status, then searches.

assertions.passing_condition: index('/Users/alice/myapp') called
status() called after index() and before search()

assertions.text: search happens only after status confirms readiness
assertions.passing_condition: search called after status confirms indexed: true

evals.name: workflow-bug-investigation
evals.prompt: Users are getting 'rate limit exceeded' errors. Find the rate limiting code, understand it, and check what recently changed.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent uses search, then explain or call-graph navigation, then commit history.

assertions.passing_condition: search called with 'rate limit' query

assertions.text: explain or call graph follows search
assertions.passing_condition: explain or find_callers called on a relevant rate limiter symbol
commit_search called with a rate-limiter-related query

evals.name: workflow-session-recovery
evals.prompt: Context was just compacted. I was working on the payment flow refactor. What was I doing?
evals.expected_behavior: Agent calls session_snapshot first, then searches archive and memory.

assertions.passing_condition: session_snapshot() is the first Contextro tool called
recall called with memory_type='archive' and payment-related query

assertions.text: agent does not restart from scratch
assertions.passing_condition: No index() call and no broad search before session_snapshot

evals.name: workflow-ast-rewrite-dry-run-first
evals.prompt: Replace all print() calls with logger.info() in src/contextro_mcp/indexing/pipeline.py. Show me what would change first.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent pattern-searches, then runs pattern_rewrite with dry_run before applying.

assertions.passing_condition: code(operation='pattern_search') called before pattern_rewrite
code(operation='pattern_rewrite', dry_run=True) called before dry_run=False
Response includes diff_preview from dry_run result before applying

evals.name: output-format-search-confidence
evals.expected_behavior: If search returns confidence low, the agent refines or switches tools instead of presenting low-confidence results as final.

assertions.passing_condition: search called
If search returns confidence=low, agent refines query or calls find_symbol

evals.name: output-format-sandbox-retrieve
evals.prompt: Find all callers of the search function.
evals.expected_behavior: If the initial result is sandboxed, the agent uses retrieve rather than claiming the preview is complete.

assertions.passing_condition: search called
If search response contains sandbox_ref, retrieve called with that ref_id

evals.name: performance-orientation-vs-baseline
evals.prompt: I just joined this project. Give me an orientation: what it does, how it's structured, and the main entry points.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent uses overview, architecture, and one or more explain calls, with materially fewer file reads than a manual baseline.

assertions.passing_condition: overview() and architecture() are called
Fewer than 3 readFile calls during orientation

evals.name: performance-safe-refactor-vs-baseline
evals.prompt: I need to add a new export parameter to the search() function. Walk me through what I need to check first.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent uses find_symbol, impact, find_callers, and explain instead of broad grep and many file reads.

assertions.text: impact is used for the refactor check
assertions.passing_condition: impact('search') called
find_callers('search') called
No grep or git log shell command before Contextro analysis

evals.name: performance-bug-investigation-vs-baseline
evals.prompt: Production is throwing 'Table already exists' errors in the memory store. Find the root cause and check if it was recently introduced.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent uses search, explain, and commit_search with fewer reads and less tool chatter than the no-skill baseline.

assertions.text: search is used for the symptom
assertions.passing_condition: search('Table already exists') or similar called

assertions.text: history search is used for regression detection
assertions.passing_condition: commit_search called with memory store or table creation query
Fewer than 3 readFile calls before root-cause summary

evals.name: workflow-modularization-orientation
evals.prompt: I need to split a growing MCP feature out of server.py. Show me the architecture and what modules or boundaries I should inspect first.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent uses architecture and targeted symbol/code explanation to understand current boundaries before recommending modularization work.

assertions.text: architecture is used for modularization context
assertions.passing_condition: architecture() called before modularization advice
No broad file-by-file read sweep before architecture or targeted symbol analysis

evals.name: performance-modular-refactor-vs-baseline
evals.prompt: Before I add a new memory feature, help me understand how to keep the code modular and avoid piling more logic into one file.
evals.expected_behavior: Agent uses architecture, explain, and impact to reason about boundaries instead of many ad hoc reads.

assertions.text: boundary analysis uses architecture and explain
assertions.passing_condition: architecture() plus explain() or impact() are used for the modularity analysis

assertions.text: manual reads stay low for modularity guidance
assertions.passing_condition: Fewer than 3 readFile calls before the modularity recommendation
